items.title: No primitive roles at project level
items.description: Avoid owner/editor/viewer; use least-privilege custom roles. Validate this in GCP projects, under IAM and Organization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: IAM and Organization: Run Prowler (`prowler gcp`) or Defender/Security Command Center for misconfig findings
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for no primitive roles at project level, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Service account key hygiene
items.description: Rotate keys, avoid user-managed keys where workload identity works. Validate this in GCP projects, under IAM and Organization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: IAM and Organization: Run Prowler (`prowler gcp`) or Defender/Security Command Center for misconfig findings
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for service account key hygiene, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for organization policies enforced, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.
